summary.notes: Pipeline has 29 CSV rows, Alex has 28 JSON rows. Pipeline includes 6 events Alex omits (Company A interest, two Target Sale events, Activist Sale, WDC Drop May 31, Bid Press Release). Alex includes 5 events pipeline omits (Final Round Inf Ann, Company D verbal indication at >$5.60, two DropTarget events for Company E and Company F, Final Round Inf deadline). Net structural overlap is 23 matched events, of which 18 are exact matches and 5 have field-level disagreements.

disagreements.filing_basis: WDC is publicly traded (listed on NASDAQ). The pipeline correctly encodes both listing status (public) and bidder kind (strategic). Alex omits the listing status qualifier.

Company H dropped after the informal round -- it could not increase its value range and was never invited to the formal final round (only WDC and Company D received final-round process letters on May 16). Pipeline correctly distinguishes this as DropAtInf (dropped at informal stage) rather than a generic Drop.

The filing says WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at $9.15 per share in cash with a mark-up of the merger agreement and drafts of ancillary agreements. This was in response to formal final-round process letters sent on May 16 requesting a response by May 28. WDC included a marked merger agreement -- a strong formal signal. Alex Formal classification is correct per filing text. Pipeline deterministic enrichment rule 1.0 mechanically applies Informal based on formality_signals (mentions_indication_of_interest=true) but this underweights the formal-round context and marked agreement.

The filing states: On May 29, 2013, our board of directors held a telephonic special meeting where the board directed BofA Merrill Lynch to request best-and-final proposals by May 30. The extension announcement date is May 29 not May 28. Pipeline is correct.

The filing says WDC submitted a written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash, described as best and final price. However, unlike the May 28 proposal, the filing does not mention a marked-up merger agreement accompanying this June 14 submission -- WDC referenced the transaction terms previously proposed on May 28. Alex classifies as Formal (best-and-final after formal round). Pipeline classifies as Informal (indication of interest language, no new merger agreement markup). Both readings are defensible.

pipeline_only_events.explanation: Alex omits Company A entirely. The filing records Company A contacting sTec on November 14, 2012, but the meeting was subsequently cancelled. Alex may have excluded it because the process never progressed to NDA. Pipeline correctly includes it as a bidder_interest event.

Alex omits the initial target_sale event in mid-November 2012. The filing says the board authorized management to contact financial advisors in mid-November 2012. Pipeline correctly captures the process initiation.

Alex omits the activist_sale event. The filing records Balch Hill filing an amended 13D on December 6, 2012, urging strategic alternatives. Alex may have excluded it because activist pressure events are not standard bid rows.

Pipeline includes a second target_sale event when the board formally authorized outreach to strategic buyers on March 26, 2013. Alex does not have a separate row for this. Both approaches are defensible.

Pipeline includes WDC May 31 withdrawal as a distinct drop event. The filing records WDC communicating it was not prepared to move forward and discontinuing due diligence. Alex skips directly from Final Round Ext (May 30) to Company D Drop (Jun 5). Pipeline is correct to include this -- it is a significant process event.
Alex omits the bid_press_release event. The filing records the June 24, 2013 joint press release announcing the signed merger. Pipeline includes it as a standard terminal event.

alex_only_rows.filing_basis: Filing confirms BofA Merrill Lynch sent process letters on April 23 requesting non-binding IOIs by May 3. This is a distinct process milestone before the May 16 formal round. Alex is correct to include it. Pipeline coverage notes incorrectly state the filing describes formal rather than informal round letters for this date.

Filing explicitly states Company D gave a verbal indication at a price greater than $5.60 per share on April 23. This is a distinct event from the May 10 written IOI at $5.75. Pipeline does not capture verbal indications as separate events.
Filing confirms Company F was excluded on April 24 for pursuing only select assets. Pipeline codes this as an exclusion in events_raw.json rather than a distinct event row. Both approaches are valid.
Filing confirms Company E was excluded for select-asset interest, shortly after April 24. Pipeline codes this as an exclusion. Date is approximate.
The May 3 deadline for non-binding IOIs is a real process milestone stated in the filing. Alex correctly captures it as the informal-round deadline.

aggregate_row_analysis.note: Alex has no explicit aggregate rows in this deal. All 28 Alex rows are individual event-level entries. The pipeline CSV likewise has no aggregate rows. No aggregate-row reconciliation is needed.

process_structural_observations: Pipeline captures 6 pre-NDA events (Company A interest, two Target Sale milestones, Activist Sale, WDC Drop May 31, Bid Press Release) that Alex omits.
Alex captures the informal-round process (April 23 process letters with May 3 deadline) as two distinct events (Final Round Inf Ann, Final Round Inf) that pipeline omits.
Alex captures Company D April 23 verbal indication at >$5.60 as a separate proposal event that pipeline omits.
Alex uses DropTarget for Company E and Company F (select-asset exits); pipeline puts these in an exclusions list rather than event rows.

The most material disagreement is bid_type for the WDC $9.15 May 28 proposal: Alex says Formal (correct per filing context -- response to formal final-round process letters with marked merger agreement), pipeline says Informal (per deterministic enrichment rules that read formality_signals mechanically). Filing evidence supports Alex.
The WDC $6.85 June 14 bid_type disagreement is genuinely ambiguous. Filing uses indication of interest language but in a best-and-final context.

recommended_corrections.basis: WDC $9.15 May 28 proposal was a response to formal final-round process letters and included a marked merger agreement. Deterministic enrichment rule 1.0 should not override the formal-round context.
Filing confirms process letters requesting non-binding IOIs by May 3. This is a distinct process milestone.
The May 3 deadline is a real process milestone. Pipeline currently skips from NDA events to proposals without marking the round structure.

recommended_corrections.recommended: Consider adding Company D verbal indication on April 23 at >$5.60
recommended_corrections.basis: Filing explicitly records this verbal indication. Whether to include verbal-only IOIs is a policy choice but the filing evidence exists.
